title: Sri Sadhu Om
meta_description: Sri Sadhu Om (d. March 1985) was a disciple of Ramana Maharshi and Tamil poet who lived near Ramanasramam for almost thirty years, compiling and elucidating Bhagavan's teachings through extensive scholarly works.
body_html:
Sri Sadhu Om lived at Sri Arunachala Ramana Nilayam located near Sri Ramanasramam, Tiruvannamalai, for almost thirty years until he left his mortal coil in 1985. His service to Ramana devotees was exemplary.

Sri Sadhu Om (Swamigal) compiled and edited Sri Ramana Jnana Bodham in verses sung extempore by the senior devotee, Sri Muruganar, which ran to nine volumes in manuscript form. Swamigal also compiled the book Sri Ramana Vazhi (The Path of Ramana) in four volumes covering Self-Enquiry, Karma and Bhakti, instructions for aspirants, and a compilation of questions and answers. He also wrote commentaries on Sri Bhagavan Ramana's Upadesa Undhiyar, Ulladu Narpadhu, and Hymns on Arunachala.

He was born in the village of Punnai Nalloor near Tanjore, South India. On completion of his schooling he joined the Revenue Department as a clerk. In his search for a Guru, he went to Sri Ramakrishna Mutt, Chennai, where the President guided him to visit Sri Ramana Maharshi.

On 6th July, 1946, Sadhu Om went to meet Sri Bhagavan. Everyone went into the hall and prostrated. When Sadhu Om went up to prostrate before Sri Bhagavan, he saw only the empty seat. Thinking Sri Bhagavan had gone out, he prostrated before the seat. When he got up, he saw a dense mass of light gradually take the form of Sri Bhagavan's physical body. In this way, Sri Bhagavan revealed His Nirguna Sath Swaroopa for Sri Swamigal on his very first visit.

Sri Bhagavan gave him repeated upadesa through the Tamil words Vandha Velaiyaip Paar — "Look after the business for which you have come" — which Sadhu Om understood as instruction to attend to the work for which he was born in this world.

In 1950, when Ramana Maharshi left his physical body, Sri Sadhu Om departed from Tiruvannamalai and stayed at Jaanaki Nilayam for five years, managing the magazine Arul. During this period he started praying for darshan of the Sage in verses. When he had composed the 172nd verse, he had a vision of Bhagavan Ramana, who said to him: 'Why do you call me to you? Why can't you come to me at my abode, Arunachala?'

Immediately after this experience, Sri Sadhu Om returned to Tiruvannamalai. He stayed there for about thirty years until he passed away in March 1985, dedicating his entire life to spreading the teachings of Sri Ramana Maharshi.

Sri Swamigal often said: "We should not run after the world; we should not look outwards at the world, we should look inwards... If we try and chase after our shadow, we will never catch it but if we go towards the sun, our shadow will automatically come running behind us."

On the evening of Thursday, March 14, 1985, Swamigal performed Arunachala girivalam and after returning home retired to bed. During the night he had a cerebral haemorrhage. His devotees returned with him to Arunachala as per his wish to shed his body in Tiruvannamalai. At 9.05 a.m. on Sunday, March 17, Swamigal peacefully breathed his last, whilst his devotees chanted the hymn Sri Aksharamanamalai. The samadhi of Sri Sadhu Om is located in a compound south of the Hill.
notes: No hero image. Page content repeats first half verbatim (Framer duplication) — kept unique content only.
